name: One South
description_short: Northwell Health's Behavioral Health College Partnership (BHCP) provides evidence-based mental health care to college and university students in acute crisis through inpatient psychiatric treatment.
description_long:
One South is a psychiatric inpatient unit operated by Northwell Health's Behavioral Health College Partnership (BHCP), now in its 15th year of operation. The program was featured in HBO's documentary "One South: Portrait of a Psych Unit" and serves as a specialized treatment facility for young adults experiencing acute mental health crises. The BHCP works with nearly 100 affiliated colleges and universities and their student counseling centers to facilitate access to care.

One South offers inpatient psychiatric treatment for college-age and young adult patients experiencing serious mental health conditions including depression, anxiety, borderline personality disorder, bipolar disorder, and psychosis. The unit is led by an interdisciplinary team of psychiatrists, licensed clinical social workers, psychologists, and behavioral health supervisors who provide comprehensive acute care. The program emphasizes evidence-based treatment approaches and coordinates closely with students' home institutions to support their return to academic success.

What distinguishes One South is its integrated college partnership model, which directly connects participating universities' student counseling centers with specialized inpatient psychiatric care. The program focuses specifically on helping college-age students achieve academic success and return to their studies following mental health crises. The unit is staffed by experienced professionals including Dr. Amanda Harris McClelland (unit chief), Dr. Laura Braider (co-founder, assistant vice president), and licensed clinical social workers with specialized expertise in young adult mental health.

One South is a healthcare service, not a consumer finance product. While the facility provides critical mental health treatment, patients should expect standard hospital billing and insurance processes. The website does not detail specific costs, financial assistance programs, or out-of-pocket expense information, which would be essential for consumers evaluating affordability.
services: Inpatient psychiatric hospitalization for acute mental health crises
Treatment of depression, anxiety, borderline personality disorder, bipolar disorder, and psychosis
Coordination with college and university student counseling centers
pros: Specialized inpatient psychiatric unit specifically designed for college-age and young adult patients
Partnership with nearly 100 affiliated colleges and universities facilitates coordinated care and academic reintegration
Interdisciplinary treatment team includes psychiatrists, clinical social workers, psychologists, and behavioral health supervisors
Expertise in treating complex conditions including OCD, psychosis, borderline personality disorder, and bipolar disorder
Evidence-based treatment approaches backed by faculty from Donald and Barbara Zucker School of Medicine
15-year track record with documented success in helping students return to graduate and lead productive lives
Featured in HBO documentary, indicating transparency and commitment to destigmatizing mental illness
cons: No financial assistance programs, payment plans, or cost transparency information disclosed on website
Requires inpatient hospitalization, which is disruptive to academic schedules and may result in substantial out-of-pocket costs
Limited geographic access—only available through Northwell Health's New York area locations (Zucker Hillside Hospital)
Website provides no information about insurance acceptance, coverage details, or what happens for uninsured/underinsured patients
Acute crisis-focused treatment; no information about ongoing outpatient care or long-term support after discharge
best_for: College and university students experiencing acute mental health crises requiring inpatient hospitalization
Young adults (ages 18-25) with serious mental illness seeking specialized psychiatric care coordinated with their school
Families of students struggling with depression, anxiety, psychosis, or personality disorders seeking evidence-based treatment
Students whose home institutions partner with Northwell's Behavioral Health College Partnership
diagnosis: One South is a specialized inpatient psychiatric treatment facility, not a consumer finance service, making the "payday-alternatives" categorization incorrect. This is best for college students and young adults in acute mental health crisis who can access Northwell Health's New York area facilities and have insurance or ability to pay hospital costs. The primary caveat is that the website provides zero information about costs, insurance acceptance, financial assistance, or affordability—critical factors for students and families considering inpatient psychiatric care.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
